account_signals.author: unknown
account_signals.text:
I think ranges/horizontal levels are the most important factor when looking at pairs.

This is because even as value of an asset changes, relative value between two assets should stay the same so long as nothing unique to one of those assets changes. In other words, asset pairs should always trade in a range until something changes that is specific to one or both parts of the pair unequally.

Let's do an example with cars and houses. If an entry level car is $30k, then likely the current entry l

search_signals.author: unknown
search_signals.text:
This is the real split in prediction markets.

US regulated platforms have clear limits.
Offshore platforms do not.

That is not a small difference.
That is the whole story.

When a market crosses the line, regulated platforms have to stop.
Offshore exchanges keep going.

So this is not just a debate about one bad contract.
It is a fight over where trust stays, where money stays, and where this market is allowed to grow.

The side that keeps this business onshore will own the real advantage.

Record put protection on S&P 500 (customer put delta at ATH negative) = investors buying max "insurance" against stock drops. High fear gauge, often contrarian bullish for equities (extremes precede rallies as hedges unwind).

Crypto tracks risk sentiment/stocks closely, so leans bullish setup for longs if stocks stabilize. Short-term volatility possible either way. Not advice.

community helps too but the chart is what catches new eyes first.
